content.claim: Published causal (not merely associational) studies isolating the effect of primary school closure on local economic outcomes (employment, investment, demographic recovery) in rural Poland are scarce or inaccessible in English-language academic databases.
content.context: Poland's structural innovations—association-school management, multi-service community hub legislation, and gmina-level governance—reduced rural primary school closures and preserved social capital compared to per-pupil-funded closure regimes, demonstrating that closure is a policy choice, not a demographic necessity, and that Hungary could achieve similar outcomes through comparable institutional design if municipal capacity and political commitment are sustained.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
